016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc.cli;
017
018import java.io.IOException;
019import java.io.InputStream;
020import java.util.logging.Level;
021import java.util.logging.LogManager;
022import java.util.logging.Logger;
023
024// TODO: Create a command-line option to set finer-grained log levels
025/**
026 * GraalVM-compatible support for using Java Logging.
027 * <p>
028 * See: <a href="https://github.com/oracle/graal/blob/master/docs/reference-manual/native-image/guides/add-logging-to-native-executable.md">add-logging-to-native-executable.md"LOGGING.md</a>
029 * <p>
030 * The default log-level for command-line tools configured in {@code logging.properties} should be {@link Level#WARNING}.
031 * The {@code -v} command-line switch should set the level to {@link Level#FINE}.
032 * Request-logging in {@link org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcClientHttpUrlConnection} is at the {@link Level#FINE} (slf4j {@code debug}) level.
033 */
034public class JavaLoggingSupport {
035    private final static String loggingPropertiesResource = "/logging.properties";
036    private static String loggerName = "";
037
038    /**
039     * Configure logging.
040     * Should be one of the first things called in `main()`
041     * @param loggerName A name for the logger.  This should be a dot-separated name
042     *     and should normally be based on the package name or class name of the subsystem
043     */
044    public static void configure(String loggerName) {
045        InputStream inputStream = BaseJsonRpcTool.class.getResourceAsStream(loggingPropertiesResource);
046        if (inputStream != null) {
047            try {
048                LogManager.getLogManager().readConfiguration(inputStream);
049            } catch (IOException e) {
050                e.printStackTrace();
051                System.err.println("JavaLoggingSupport: failed to process " + loggingPropertiesResource);
052            }
053        } else {
054            System.err.println("JavaLoggingSupport: failed to load " + loggingPropertiesResource);
055        }
056        JavaLoggingSupport.loggerName = loggerName;
057    }
058
059    /**
060     * Change log level (eg. as a result of `-v` command-line option)
061     * This sets the level to {@link Level#FINE}
062     */
063    public static void setVerbose() {
064        setLogLevel(Level.FINE);
065    }
066
067    /**
068     * Change log level (e.g. as a result of `-log=level` command-line option)
069     * @param level {@code java.util.logging} log level
070     */
071    public static void setLogLevel(Level level) {
072        final Logger app = Logger.getLogger(loggerName);
073        app.setLevel(level);
074    }
075
076}
